name: SEPTUM PELLUCIDUM
definition: 1.5-3.0 mm thin vertical membrane of triangular shape (when viewed from side) persistence / failure of fusion of potential space (= slitlike cavity = 5th ventricle) between "6th ventricle" fluid-filled cavity located posterior to a vertical plane formed by columns of fornix

demographics.Frequency: in 80% of term infants; in 15% of adults
demographics.Location: posterior to genu of corpus callosum, inferior to body of corpus callosum,

distribution: posterior to genu of corpus callosum, inferior to body of corpus callosum,
discriminating_features: posterior midline continuation of cavum septi pellucidi beyond foramen of Monro (=

general: posterior midline continuation of cavum septi pellucidi beyond foramen of Monro (=
